scenarios.scenario-2: No live calls result is trusted if the SSH-forward or SOCKS5 handoff is still ambiguous.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The handoff must tell the operator to keep SSH-forward liveness evidence separate from UDP media diagnosis.

scenarios.scenario-1: One operator voice call produces signaling evidence plus UDP association and media-path evidence.
scenarios.scenario-2: Voice-call failure is classified at the first missing UDP layer instead of being reduced to a generic Telegram symptom.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: A voice-call packet must keep signaling evidence separate from UDP media evidence.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Voice success is not inferred from ringing or answered-call UI without the UDP markers.

scenarios.scenario-1: One operator video call produces its own setup and media evidence packet.
scenarios.scenario-2: Video-call failure remains separate from the voice result so a narrower media defect can be isolated.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Video-call evidence must remain separate from the voice-call packet rather than inheriting the voice result.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Video success is not inferred from a green voice call without its own media markers.

scenarios.scenario-1: A second call attempt yields evidence for a new UDP association after the first call ends.
scenarios.scenario-2: Reconnect evidence rejects stale association reuse and points at the lifecycle layer if it occurs.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The reconnect packet must show a fresh association lifecycle after the prior call ends or idles out.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Reused stale relay state is a failure, not a reconnect success.

scenarios.scenario-1: The calls wave stores separate packets for call setup, media flow, reconnect, and failure classification.
scenarios.scenario-2: A blended transcript without the separate packet boundaries is treated as insufficient evidence.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The packet set must remain split into voice, video, reconnect, and failure evidence instead of one blended manual summary.

scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision is anchored to the tested Telegram Desktop setup only.
scenarios.scenario-2: The live calls phase rejects any broader claim than the tested setup and captured evidence support.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The final decision must separately state the bounded result for voice, video, and reconnect behavior if they diverge.

scenarios.scenario-1: The repaired baseline containing commit 5d8b598 is redeployed to both live hosts through the governed deploy path.
scenarios.scenario-2: The rerun wave stops if the repaired baseline is not what reaches the live hosts.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The rerun deploy packet must distinguish the repaired baseline from the older pre-fix Phase-19 deploy state.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The rerun deploy wording must stay bounded to the ingress repair and must not imply that working Telegram calls are already proven.

scenarios.scenario-1: Post-fix readiness proves the live listener no longer rejects UDP ASSOCIATE before the governed control session starts.
scenarios.scenario-2: If UDP ASSOCIATE still never appears, the rerun stops at readiness rather than reusing the old call packet blindly.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The post-fix readiness packet must explicitly prove whether the first divergent layer moved beyond UDP ingress.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The readiness packet must keep generic TCP signaling readiness separate from repaired UDP ingress proof.

scenarios.scenario-1: The repaired voice rerun yields a new bounded packet that either reaches UDP ingress or classifies a deeper first divergent block.
scenarios.scenario-2: The repaired voice rerun must not silently reuse the original Phase-19 no-go packet.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The repaired voice rerun must classify the first missing post-fix media marker instead of collapsing back to a generic key-exchange symptom.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: If BLOCK_HANDLE_UDP_ASSOCIATE appears, the packet must promote the next missing datagram marker to the first divergent block.

scenarios.scenario-1: The repaired video rerun yields its own bounded packet after the ingress fix.
scenarios.scenario-2: The repaired video rerun cannot inherit the repaired voice result without its own packetized evidence.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The repaired video rerun must remain separately classified even if the repaired voice rerun is still no-go.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Video rerun evidence must explicitly say whether failure matched the repaired voice divergent layer or diverged later.

scenarios.scenario-1: The rerun packet set stores deploy, readiness, voice, video, and updated first-divergent-block evidence separately from Phase-19.
scenarios.scenario-2: A blended summary that erases the pre-fix versus post-fix boundary is rejected as insufficient evidence.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The rerun evidence packet must preserve the distinction between the original no-go packet and the repaired rerun results.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The rerun packet must remain split into deploy, readiness, voice, video, and failure sections rather than only a post-fix prose summary.

scenarios.scenario-1: The updated decision states exactly what changed after the repaired rerun and remains bounded to the tested setup.
scenarios.scenario-2: The rerun decision must not overclaim working calls if the repaired wave only moved the divergent block deeper.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The updated decision must compare against the old Phase-19 no-go packet without silently overwriting it.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The updated decision must state separately whether the ingress defect is fixed even if end-to-end call success is still not proven.

scenarios.scenario-1: The new phase explicitly separates the local-client topology from the older SSH-forwarded Desktop path.
scenarios.scenario-2: The topology profile is rejected if it silently reuses the older operator path without comparison semantics.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The profile must explicitly state that the old SSH-forwarded Desktop path remains historical evidence rather than the new execution target.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The profile must keep the future claim bounded to the tested workstation build, tested server pair, and tested network setup only.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'local n0wss-client|127.0.0.1:1080|without ssh -L|local workstation' docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml docs/knowledge-graph.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The runbook shape makes Telegram Desktop point at a truly local listener instead of an SSH-forwarded remote one.
scenarios.scenario-2: The runbook is rejected if it leaves the operator topology ambiguous.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The runbook must explicitly forbid keeping the old ssh -L path enabled during the local-client wave.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The runbook must specify how the operator proves the listener is local before Telegram is started.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'local bootstrap|trust anchor|local n0wss-client|cleanup' docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml docs/knowledge-graph.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The local bootstrap shape defines binary, env, trust material, launch, and cleanup surfaces explicitly.
scenarios.scenario-2: The local bootstrap is rejected if it relies on ad hoc workstation mutation with no bounded launch shape.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The bootstrap must define provenance for the local binary and trust material so the future wave can distinguish workstation drift from runtime behavior.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The bootstrap must define a cleanup shape so local reruns do not reuse stale processes or stale listeners.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'local readiness|UDP ASSOCIATE|local listener|local smoke' docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml docs/knowledge-graph.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The local-client topology requires proof of local listener, local smoke, and local UDP ASSOCIATE readiness before any call attempt.
scenarios.scenario-2: The wave stops if the local workstation path is not proven ready before operator calls start.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The readiness packet must show a raw local UDP ASSOCIATE proof on the same workstation topology that Telegram will use.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: If local readiness is green, later call failure may not be attributed back to generic listener ingress without new contrary evidence.

scenarios.scenario-1: The local-client voice wave captures signaling and media evidence separately.
scenarios.scenario-2: The local-client voice wave must not inherit evidence from the older SSH-forwarded Desktop packet.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The voice packet must classify signaling green and media green separately for the local topology.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: If local UDP ingress is proven but later datagram markers are missing, the packet must promote the next missing marker to the new first divergent block.

scenarios.scenario-1: The local-client video wave remains packetized separately from the local voice wave.
scenarios.scenario-2: Video cannot silently inherit the local voice result.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The video packet must state whether its first divergent layer matches or differs from the local voice packet.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: UI ringing or answer state alone is not valid media success evidence for the local topology.

scenarios.scenario-1: The local-client packet set preserves readiness, voice, video, and failure sections separately.
scenarios.scenario-2: A blended local summary with no topology comparison is rejected as insufficient evidence.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The evidence set must preserve both the older SSH-forwarded packet and the new local-client packet without collapsing them into one historical thread.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The evidence set must keep room for a later external-network hypothesis only after local and governed path markers are classified.

scenarios.scenario-1: The comparison packet explicitly contrasts the old SSH-forwarded path with the new local-client path.
scenarios.scenario-2: The comparison is rejected if it erases the older no-go packet or fails to isolate topology dependence.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The comparison packet must explicitly state whether the new result changes because the topology changed or because the deeper datagram path changed.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Provider or network blocking may be raised only after the comparison packet shows no earlier topology or governed-path divergence.

scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision stays bounded to the tested local-client topology and explicitly compares against the older SSH-forwarded path.
scenarios.scenario-2: The final decision must not overclaim universal Telegram calls success or solve all network blocking regimes.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The final decision must state separately whether the local topology changed the evidence envelope at all.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: If all governed markers are green and calls still fail, only then may the decision classify the remaining risk as likely external network filtering or provider-side blocking.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'hypothesis|Telegram Desktop|external filtering|remote media|SOCKS5 UDP' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The hypothesis set remains bounded to app behavior, governed datagram path, and external filtering only after earlier layers are classified.
scenarios.scenario-2: The phase must not jump straight to provider or regulator blocking without controlled-probe evidence.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The hypothesis set must preserve the already proven facts from Phase-21 instead of reopening topology questions as unresolved.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The hypothesis set must keep app behavior, datagram-path defects, and external filtering as distinct branches rather than a blended “network problem” bucket.

scenarios.scenario-1: Controlled tooling can open SOCKS5 UDP ASSOCIATE and send bounded datagrams without Telegram UI.
scenarios.scenario-2: The probe path must not blur success of raw association with success of end-to-end datagram exchange.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The tooling must preserve the UDP ASSOCIATE marker and at least one deeper datagram marker in its success path.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The tooling must leave an evidence shape that can distinguish association success, outbound datagram success, and inbound reply success as separate stages.

scenarios.scenario-1: The capture surface is bounded by time and location so app and tunnel traffic can be compared without noisy unbounded dumps.
scenarios.scenario-2: Capture guidance must not rely on unconstrained workstation-wide packet logs that are too noisy to classify.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Capture commands must preserve correlation between workstation events and n0wss runtime markers.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Capture guidance must name the minimal interface, duration, and packet scope needed for later comparison against controlled probes.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'app behavior|bypass|proxy-governed UDP|stalls before media' docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml README.md

scenarios.scenario-1: The classifier distinguishes proxy-governed UDP usage from bypass or pre-media stalling behavior.
scenarios.scenario-2: The classifier must not report a transport-core defect if the app never attempts the governed media path.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Classification must compare workstation capture evidence with runtime markers instead of relying on Telegram UI text alone.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The classifier must explicitly say whether Telegram produced proxy-governed UDP, bypass traffic, or no media-path attempt at all.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'remote media|UDP relay|echo target|inbound|outbound' docs/OPERATORS.md docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The remote relay path is exercised independently of Telegram and captures outbound and inbound datagram evidence.
scenarios.scenario-2: A remote-media probe must not be mistaken for Telegram app success.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The probe must preserve outbound and inbound relay markers separately so asymmetric failures stay visible.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The probe packet must remain independent from Telegram Desktop symptoms so relay reachability is not inferred from UI behavior.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'blocking boundary|external filtering|app behavior mismatch|datagram-path defect' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The boundary packet distinguishes app behavior, governed-path defects, and only then plausible external filtering.
scenarios.scenario-2: Provider-blocking language must not appear before controlled probes and app classification are complete.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The boundary packet must point to the first still-unresolved layer rather than repeat already-closed topology questions.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: External filtering may be elevated only if both the controlled probe and the remote media probe leave no earlier unresolved governed-path gap.

module-checks.check-1: rg -n 'next-step|next architecture|workaround phase|bounded decision' README.md docs/development-plan.xml docs/verification-plan.xml

scenarios.scenario-1: The final decision names the next architectural phase without overstating certainty beyond the evidence packet.
scenarios.scenario-2: The final decision must not claim universal provider blocking or universal Telegram incompatibility without bounded proof.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The decision must say whether the next phase is transport, app-specific handoff, or external-network workaround oriented.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The decision must preserve the bounded reason why that next phase is needed, using the blocking-boundary packet rather than a generic “calls still do not work” summary.

LV-001.NAME: RemoteAccessPreparation
LV-001.goal: Prepare both clean hosts without storing credentials in repository artifacts.

required-evidence.evidence-1: The same release binary or checksum is staged for both hosts.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Host roles are fixed: one machine serves WSS, the other serves client SOCKS5 ingress.
required-evidence.evidence-3: No command transcript or log artifact stores the SSH password or raw auth token.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The server host exposes bash, ss, and openssl, and the client host exposes bash, ss, and curl before live staging begins.

LV-001.failure-packet-template: scenario=RemoteAccessPreparation; expected-evidence=both hosts reachable and identified; observed-evidence=ssh or staging failure output; first-divergent-block=HOST_CONNECTIVITY; next-action=repair ssh access or package staging before live launch

LV-002.NAME: TwoHostSmokeExecution
LV-002.goal: Run the first deterministic two-host smoke with one success path and one auth rejection path.

required-evidence.evidence-1: VF-LIVE-001, VF-LIVE-002, VF-LIVE-003, and VF-LIVE-005 each produce their required log markers or trace sequence evidence.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The smoke request returns deterministic output metadata such as HTTP status, response headers, or a checksum of a known small payload.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The success-path evidence packet includes the command exit code, bounded log tails, and bound-socket state from both hosts.

LV-002.failure-packet-template: scenario=TwoHostSmokeExecution; expected-evidence=live startup, one successful proxy request, and clean shutdown; observed-evidence=log excerpts, command exit codes, socket state, and remote process state; first-divergent-block=first missing required marker; next-action=repair the failing module before repeating the live wave

LV-003.NAME: LiveAuthRejectExecution
LV-003.goal: Run one explicit bad-auth attempt after the success path and prove the server rejects it without creating a live session.

required-evidence.evidence-1: VF-LIVE-004 produces the auth validation anchor and a deterministic reject trajectory.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The bad-auth attempt leaves no surviving session on the server after the failed request.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The failure is observable without exposing the invalid token value in logs or saved command transcripts.

LV-003.failure-packet-template: scenario=LiveAuthRejectExecution; expected-evidence=explicit bad-auth attempt rejected with redacted evidence and no surviving session; observed-evidence=server log tail, client log tail, bad-auth command exit code, and session or socket state; first-divergent-block=first missing reject-path marker; next-action=repair auth or handshake handling before any further live rerun

LV-004.NAME: LiveEvidenceCollection
LV-004.goal: Collect narrow high-signal artifacts from both hosts so another agent can diagnose failure without rerunning blind.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Logs include only anchored high-signal lines and redact secrets.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Socket listings prove whether listeners were bound at the moment of success or failure.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Captured evidence is small enough for a follow-up fix packet and identifies the first divergent block.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The evidence bundle contains separate success-path and bad-auth-path artifacts so the two trajectories are not conflated.

LV-004.failure-packet-template: scenario=LiveEvidenceCollection; expected-evidence=both host logs plus socket state plus smoke result; observed-evidence=missing or noisy artifacts; first-divergent-block=EVIDENCE_COLLECTION; next-action=improve log capture or restart with explicit output paths

LV-005.NAME: ManagedServiceWavePreparation
LV-005.goal: Prepare the managed deployment shape for the second live wave without falling back to ad hoc host mutation.

required-evidence.evidence-1: The canonical deploy layout, unit names, env sources, and log targets are fixed before managed live verification starts.
required-evidence.evidence-2: No approved command requires raw secret material to be embedded in repository files.

LV-005.failure-packet-template: scenario=ManagedServiceWavePreparation; expected-evidence=deploy script, units, and secret inputs are aligned; observed-evidence=dry-run, static verify, or doc drift failure; first-divergent-block=managed prep artifact mismatch; next-action=repair deploy layout, unit files, or secret inputs before service-managed live execution

LV-006.NAME: ManagedServiceWaveExecution
LV-006.goal: Run the second live wave under managed services with repeated smoke, restart recovery, and bounded bad-auth evidence.

commands.command-1: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'systemctl restart n0wss-server && systemctl is-active n0wss-server'
commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'systemctl restart n0wss-client && systemctl is-active n0wss-client'

required-evidence.evidence-1: VF-DEPLOY-002, VF-DEPLOY-003, VF-DEPLOY-004, VF-DEPLOY-005, and VF-DEPLOY-006 each produce their required evidence.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Repeated smoke and restart evidence are captured separately from the bad-auth evidence packet.

LV-006.failure-packet-template: scenario=ManagedServiceWaveExecution; expected-evidence=service-managed readiness, repeated smoke success, restart recovery, and redacted bad-auth reject; observed-evidence=journal, systemctl, socket, and output failure details; first-divergent-block=first missing managed-service marker; next-action=repair service, deploy, or runtime behavior before rerun

LV-007.NAME: TelegramProxyWave
LV-007.goal: Run one Telegram-specific compatibility wave through the governed SOCKS5 endpoint and capture bounded connect plus reconnect evidence.

commands.command-3: if Telegram runs on the local operator workstation, start and keep alive `ssh -N -L 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT:127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST` before the app-side check
commands.command-4: if Telegram runs on the local operator workstation, prove the forwarded local bind with `ss -ltnp | grep \":$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT\" || true` before the app-side check
commands.command-5: telegram-client-side configure SOCKS5 proxy 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT and attempt initial connect
commands.command-6: collect bounded client and server log tails for the initial connect packet
commands.command-7: restart the Telegram app or trigger one reconnect path through the same SOCKS5 settings
commands.command-8: if reconnect stalls at Connecting on the local workstation, verify the SSH-forward process is still alive before classifying a tunnel-side failure
commands.command-9: collect bounded client and server log tails for the reconnect packet

required-evidence.evidence-1: A Telegram app instance is configured as a standard SOCKS5 client against the local n0wss endpoint.
required-evidence.evidence-2: For the local-workstation variant, the packet includes proof of the SSH local forward and the forwarded local bind, not only the remote managed listener.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The success packet contains SOCKS5 parse, transport selection, and pump markers or a bounded failure packet with the first divergent block.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Reconnect evidence is captured separately from the initial connect attempt.
required-evidence.evidence-5: If the app attempt never reaches n0wss, the packet must explicitly classify the divergence as app-side misconfiguration, forward-liveness failure, or readiness failure rather than tunnel failure.
required-evidence.evidence-6: When reconnect succeeds but a local relaunch attempt shows unstable UI recovery, the packet keeps the reconnect-path success evidence separate from the app-launch divergence note.

LV-007.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramProxyWave; expected-evidence=Telegram connects through SOCKS5 and survives one reconnect path with forward proof when the client is local; observed-evidence=Telegram app result, forward state, local listener state, and anchored n0wss logs; first-divergent-block=first missing forward, SOCKS5, or transport marker; next-action=check SSH-forward liveness first for local-workstation runs, then distinguish Telegram misconfig from tunnel-side failure before rerun

LV-008.NAME: FinalTelegramAcceptanceWave
LV-008.goal: Redeploy onto the rebuilt server pair, prove managed readiness, hand off one exact Telegram Desktop path to the operator, and capture separate basic and media-heavy acceptance packets.

commands.command-1: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'
commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'
commands.command-4: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'systemctl is-active n0wss-server && systemctl show n0wss-server -p MainPID --no-pager'
commands.command-5: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'systemctl is-active n0wss-client && systemctl show n0wss-client -p MainPID --no-pager'
commands.command-8: operator starts and keeps alive `ssh -N -L 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT:127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST` on the local workstation
commands.command-9: operator proves the forwarded local bind with `ss -ltnp | grep \":$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT\" || true` before Telegram Desktop testing
commands.command-10: operator configures Telegram Desktop to use SOCKS5 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT with no username and no password
commands.command-11: operator performs a basic-connect packet and at least one text or small-message action
commands.command-12: operator performs media-heavy checks as available: photo send, large file send, voice call, and video call
commands.command-13: if Telegram stalls at Connecting or a media path fails, verify SSH-forward liveness before classifying a tunnel-side failure
commands.command-14: capture separate bounded server and client log tails after basic-connect and after media-heavy actions

required-evidence.evidence-1: The fresh-host packet proves SSH reachability, OS identity, and baseline tooling on both rebuilt Ubuntu hosts before deploy.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The deploy packet proves the governed managed path was reapplied to both hosts with no ad hoc drift.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The readiness packet proves active services, bound listeners, and startup anchors before the operator starts Telegram testing.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The handoff packet contains the exact SSH-forward command, SOCKS5 parameters, and the moment the operator was told to begin.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The basic-connect packet is separate from photo, large-file, voice-call, and video-call observations.
required-evidence.evidence-6: Any failure packet identifies whether the first divergence was host baseline, deploy, readiness, SSH-forward, SOCKS5 ingress, transport selection, or a media-specific path.
required-evidence.evidence-7: The final go/no-go decision remains explicitly bounded to Telegram Desktop on the rebuilt host pair.
required-evidence.evidence-8: The acceptance record preserves the March 29, 2026 outcome that text, photo, and large-file paths were green while call attempts stalled at Telegram key exchange.

LV-008.failure-packet-template: scenario=FinalTelegramAcceptanceWave; expected-evidence=fresh-host baseline, managed readiness, explicit SSH-forward handoff, basic-connect packet, and separate media packets; observed-evidence=host baseline output, deploy output, service state, forward state, Telegram operator result, and anchored server-client logs; first-divergent-block=first missing host-baseline, readiness, SSH-forward, SOCKS5, transport, or media marker; next-action=repair the first divergent layer before repeating only the dependent acceptance step

LV-009.NAME: TelegramCallsWave
LV-009.goal: Run a dedicated Telegram Desktop voice or video calls wave through SOCKS5 UDP ASSOCIATE and the governed UDP-capable tunnel path.

commands.command-3: operator starts and keeps alive `ssh -N -L 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT:127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST` for the local-workstation Telegram Desktop path
commands.command-4: operator proves the local forwarded bind exists with `ss -ltnp | grep \":$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT\" || true` before opening the call
commands.command-5: operator configures Telegram Desktop to use SOCKS5 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT and initiates one voice or video call
commands.command-6: capture client and server logs for SOCKS5 UDP ASSOCIATE, datagram session open, datagram transport selection, WSS datagram, and UDP relay markers
commands.command-7: capture separate packets for call-setup, media-flow, reconnect, and failure evidence
commands.command-8: end the first call, then trigger one fresh reconnect call attempt through the same SOCKS5 settings
commands.command-9: if the call stalls, separate signaling evidence from UDP media evidence before rerun

required-evidence.evidence-1: Telegram signaling remains green on the existing TCP path while UDP ASSOCIATE creates a governed relay bind.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Outbound and inbound UDP media packets produce bounded datagram transport and relay evidence.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Failure packets classify the first divergent layer as UDP ingress, datagram session, transport selection, remote relay, or app-side behavior.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Reconnect evidence shows whether a new UDP association was opened after the prior call ended or idled out.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The calls wave keeps the SSH-forward liveness proof for the local-workstation variant separate from the UDP media diagnosis.

LV-009.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramCallsWave; expected-evidence=signaling green, UDP ASSOCIATE green, and bounded media-path evidence for one call; observed-evidence=Telegram call result, UDP association state, datagram transport logs, relay logs, and reconnect outcome; first-divergent-block=first missing UDP ASSOCIATE, datagram transport, relay, or inbound media marker; next-action=repair the first divergent layer before replaying the calls wave

LV-010.NAME: TelegramCallsLiveAcceptanceWave
LV-010.goal: Redeploy the UDP-capable baseline to the fresh host pair, hand off one exact Telegram Desktop route to the operator, and collect separate voice, video, reconnect, and failure packets for the live calls setup.

commands.command-1: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'
commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'
commands.command-6: operator starts and keeps alive `ssh -N -L 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT:127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST`
commands.command-7: operator proves the local forwarded bind with `ss -ltnp | grep \":$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT\" || true` before starting the first call
commands.command-8: operator configures Telegram Desktop to use SOCKS5 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT with no username and no password
commands.command-9: operator runs one voice call and one video call as separate attempts
commands.command-10: capture client and server logs for signaling, UDP ASSOCIATE, datagram transport, WSS datagram, and UDP relay markers during each attempt
commands.command-11: end the first call and run one fresh second call attempt to capture reconnect evidence
commands.command-12: if any call stalls, classify the first missing layer before rerun and keep SSH-forward liveness separate from UDP diagnosis

required-evidence.evidence-1: The live wave yields a fresh-host baseline packet before deployment or app-side interpretation begins.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The readiness packet proves active services, bound listener state, and UDP-capable runtime anchors before the operator starts Telegram calls testing.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The handoff packet contains the exact SSH-forward command, SOCKS5 parameters, and the ordered call-check sequence.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Voice and video results are kept as separate setup or media packets and are not blended.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The reconnect packet shows whether a fresh UDP association was opened after the prior call ended or idled out.
required-evidence.evidence-6: The failure packet identifies the first divergent layer as SSH-forward, UDP ASSOCIATE, datagram session, transport selection, relay, or app-side behavior.
required-evidence.evidence-7: The final go-or-no-go decision remains explicitly bounded to Telegram Desktop on the tested fresh host pair.
required-evidence.evidence-8: Voice and video outcomes remain separately reportable even if one succeeds and the other fails.

LV-010.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramCallsLiveAcceptanceWave; expected-evidence=fresh-host baseline, managed readiness, explicit SSH-forward handoff, separate voice and video packets, reconnect packet, and bounded failure packet; observed-evidence=host baseline output, service state, forward state, Telegram call result, and anchored client-server logs; first-divergent-block=first missing readiness, SSH-forward, UDP ASSOCIATE, datagram transport, relay, or app-side marker; next-action=repair the first divergent layer before repeating only the dependent call step

LV-011.NAME: TelegramCallsLiveRerunAfterIngressFix
LV-011.goal: Redeploy the repaired UDP ASSOCIATE ingress baseline, prove post-fix readiness, and collect a new bounded Telegram calls rerun packet.

commands.command-5: operator reruns one Telegram Desktop voice call through the same SSH-forward and SOCKS5 path
commands.command-6: operator reruns one Telegram Desktop video call through the same SSH-forward and SOCKS5 path
commands.command-7: collect bounded client and server log tails separately for post-fix voice and post-fix video attempts

required-evidence.evidence-1: The rerun packet is explicitly post-fix and tied to the repaired baseline rather than the older Phase-19 deploy.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The rerun proves whether BLOCK_HANDLE_UDP_ASSOCIATE now appears in live logs.
required-evidence.evidence-3: If the first divergent block moved forward, the packet identifies the first missing deeper datagram marker.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Voice and video rerun packets remain separate.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The rerun packet states explicitly whether the ingress defect is fixed even if calls remain no-go overall.

LV-011.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramCallsLiveRerunAfterIngressFix; expected-evidence=post-fix readiness plus a new voice and video rerun packet; observed-evidence=deploy provenance, service state, operator symptom, and bounded log tails; first-divergent-block=first missing post-fix UDP ingress, datagram transport, or relay marker; next-action=repair the first post-fix divergent layer before another live rerun

LV-012.NAME: TelegramCallsLocalClientTopologyWave
LV-012.goal: Run a dedicated Telegram calls wave through a truly local n0wss-client topology and compare its evidence against the older SSH-forwarded Desktop path.

commands.command-1: launch n0wss-client locally on the Telegram Desktop workstation with the bounded local bootstrap profile
commands.command-2: prove the local SOCKS5 listener exists on 127.0.0.1:$N0WSS_SOCKS5_PORT
commands.command-3: run one local raw SOCKS5 UDP ASSOCIATE probe before Telegram calls begin
commands.command-4: operator runs one Telegram Desktop voice call through the local-client topology
commands.command-5: operator runs one Telegram Desktop video call through the local-client topology
commands.command-6: collect bounded local client logs plus bounded remote server logs for both call attempts
commands.command-7: compare the new local-client packet against the older SSH-forwarded no-go packet before issuing any network-block explanation

required-evidence.evidence-1: The local-client topology packet is kept separate from the older SSH-forwarded packet.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The local wave proves local listener readiness and local UDP ASSOCIATE readiness before Telegram is used.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Voice and video local-client packets remain separate.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The final packet compares whether calls behavior changed only because the topology changed.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The wave states explicitly whether any remaining failure is still inside the governed path or only plausibly external to it.

LV-012.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramCallsLocalClientTopologyWave; expected-evidence=local listener proof, local UDP ASSOCIATE proof, separate voice and video packets, and a topology comparison packet; observed-evidence=local bootstrap state, local operator symptom, bounded local client logs, bounded remote server logs, and any governed UDP markers; first-divergent-block=first missing local readiness, local UDP ingress, datagram transport, relay, or app-side marker; next-action=repair only the first divergent local-topology layer before another rerun

LV-013.NAME: TelegramCallsRootCauseIsolationWave
LV-013.goal: Run controlled datagram probes, bounded packet capture, and Telegram Desktop classification so the remaining calls blocker is isolated before any new transport or workaround phase.

commands.command-2: capture a bounded workstation packet or socket trace during one Telegram Desktop voice-call attempt
commands.command-3: capture bounded client and server log tails covering UDP ASSOCIATE, datagram transport, and relay markers during the same attempt
commands.command-4: run one remote media reachability probe through the governed UDP relay path independent of Telegram UI
commands.command-5: assemble a bounded comparison packet covering controlled probe, live app behavior, and remote media reachability

required-evidence.evidence-1: The controlled probe proves or falsifies end-to-end datagram reachability independently of Telegram Desktop.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The workstation capture proves whether Telegram Desktop emits media-path traffic through the proxy-governed path.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The remote media probe proves or falsifies relay reachability independently of Telegram UI state.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The final packet separates app behavior mismatch, datagram-path defect, and plausible external filtering.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The wave keeps controlled-probe, live-app, and remote-media packets separate so the first unresolved layer can be promoted cleanly.

LV-013.failure-packet-template: scenario=TelegramCallsRootCauseIsolationWave; expected-evidence=controlled datagram probe, bounded workstation capture, remote media probe, and bounded root-cause packet; observed-evidence=probe output, capture evidence, runtime markers, and relay evidence; first-divergent-block=first missing controlled-probe, app-behavior, or remote-media marker; next-action=repair or narrow the first unresolved layer before any new workaround phase

Gate-Phase-1.goal: Foundation modules have deterministic local checks for config, redaction, metrics, auth, and TLS setup.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Startup, metrics, auth, and TLS boundaries have module-local checks defined.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Phase-1 anchors exist for BLOCK_VALIDATE_CONFIGURATION, BLOCK_INIT_TRACING, BLOCK_VALIDATE_HANDSHAKE, BLOCK_LOAD_TLS_MATERIAL, and BLOCK_START_APPLICATION.
required-evidence.evidence-3: No Phase-1 module relies on integration-only transports or end-to-end tunnel setup to prove its local contract.

Gate-Phase-2.goal: Both transport boundaries satisfy the shared adapter cleanup and task-tracking contracts.

required-evidence.evidence-1: WSS and iroh adapter contract tests pass without leaked tracked tasks.

Gate-Phase-3.goal: Session orchestration, typed effect dispatch, fallback, and state transitions are explicit and verifiable.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Registry, selector, pure transitions, and stable top-level effect routing are automated and green.

Gate-Phase-4.goal: SOCKS5 ingress produces bounded ProxyIntent work items and accurate reply codes.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Bounded queue semantics, deterministic request parsing, and exhaustive reply mapping are covered.

Gate-Phase-5.goal: The bridge carries real traffic over transport-agnostic streams.

required-evidence.evidence-1: At least one end-to-end SOCKS5-over-tunnel scenario is automated and green.

Gate-Phase-6.goal: Fallback, shutdown, timing, burst handling, and observability are robust enough for broader execution waves.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Controlled shutdown, queue pressure, session limit pressure, sliding-window burst detection, and transport fallback scenarios produce stable evidence.

Gate-Phase-7.goal: The repository is release-ready for GitHub publication and first-round smoke testing.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Markup debt is removed, release docs are aligned, CI is current, the release binary is emitted, and the smoke path passes.

Gate-Live-1.goal: The first two-host live environment proves remote readiness, one successful proxied request, one deterministic auth rejection, and clean shutdown.

required-evidence.evidence-1: VF-LIVE-001 proves server readiness with config, TLS, and startup anchors.
required-evidence.evidence-2: VF-LIVE-002 proves client readiness with config and startup anchors.
required-evidence.evidence-3: VF-LIVE-003 proves one successful proxied request with transport-selection and pump evidence.
required-evidence.evidence-4: VF-LIVE-004 proves invalid auth is rejected with redacted server-side evidence.
required-evidence.evidence-5: VF-LIVE-005 proves shutdown leaves no live listeners or stuck session cleanup.

Gate-Phase-9.goal: The project is ready for repeatable managed deployment, bounded evidence capture, and a second live verification wave.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Deploy layout, secret handling, deploy scripting, systemd units, and runbook stay aligned.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Managed startup, restart recovery, repeated smoke, and bad-auth checks produce bounded evidence suitable for another agent to debug.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Managed-service evidence includes both system-manager state and runtime anchors, with separate packets for success, restart, and reject trajectories.

Gate-Phase-10.goal: The repository is ready to tag and publish the source-first v0.3.0 release without dirty-tree blockers or release-note drift.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Governed test-file cleanup, release-facing docs, and the v0.3.0 changelog are committed and aligned.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The next release version is explicitly justified against the remote tag baseline.
required-evidence.evidence-3: GRACE review, refresh, and status all support the release-ready decision.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The final release-ready commit has no tracked dirty files and no whitespace or patch-integrity defects.

Gate-Phase-11.goal: The project has bounded Telegram-specific compatibility evidence without overstating unblock guarantees.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Telegram-facing profile, readiness path, smoke wave, and evidence packet shape are documented and aligned.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Telegram compatibility is documented as SOCKS5 proxy support over the existing tunnel path, not as a new protocol.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Compatibility claims stay inside the verified evidence envelope.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Phase evidence includes separable readiness, initial-connect, reconnect, and bounded-failure packets rather than one blended manual transcript.

Gate-Phase-12.goal: The proven local Telegram Desktop path is operator-reproducible because the SSH-forward prerequisite is explicit in both the runbook and verification contract.

commands.command-1: rg -n 'ssh -N -L 127.0.0.1:1080:127.0.0.1:1080|Telegram Desktop|local workstation|remote client host' docs/OPERATORS.md
commands.command-3: rg -n 'M-TELEGRAM-FORWARD-|forwarded Desktop|SSH local forward' docs/knowledge-graph.xml docs/development-plan.xml

required-evidence.evidence-1: The operator runbook makes the local-forward prerequisite explicit for the verified Desktop scenario.
required-evidence.evidence-2: LV-007 mirrors the same prerequisite instead of assuming a local listener exists by itself.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The knowledge graph stays synchronized with the clarified Telegram Desktop operator path.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The failure contract explicitly checks SSH-forward liveness before a local-workstation Connecting-only symptom can be blamed on the tunnel runtime.

Gate-Phase-13.goal: The repository is ready to create and push the v0.3.1 patch-release tag after the Telegram forward clarification wave.

required-evidence.evidence-1: The changelog contains a v0.3.1 entry for the Telegram forward clarification patch scope.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Release-facing docs stay aligned with the verified forwarded Telegram Desktop path.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The local tree is clean and patch-integrity checks pass before tag creation.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The remote baseline already contains v0.3.0 and the next tag is justified as v0.3.1.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The existing GitHub workflow remains valid for the pushed v0.3.1 tag.
required-evidence.evidence-6: No stale local v0.3.1 tag exists before the approved release-push step.

Gate-Phase-14.goal: The rebuilt two-host environment is freshly deployed, operator-ready, and yields a bounded final Telegram Desktop acceptance packet.

commands.command-1: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'
commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'

required-evidence.evidence-1: Both rebuilt Ubuntu hosts produce a fresh-host baseline packet before the governed deploy path starts.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Both rebuilt Ubuntu hosts are redeployed and green before the operator Telegram test starts.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The operator receives one exact SSH-forward plus SOCKS5 handoff for Telegram Desktop and proves the local forwarded bind before app testing.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Basic Telegram acceptance and media-heavy scenarios are recorded as separable packets.
required-evidence.evidence-5: A first-divergent-block discipline exists if any Telegram scenario fails, and SSH-forward liveness is checked before tunnel blame on local-workstation runs.
required-evidence.evidence-6: The final go/no-go decision is bounded to the tested Telegram Desktop setup on the rebuilt hosts.

Gate-Phase-15.goal: The repository is ready to publish the current local baseline as v0.3.2 before the UDP calls architecture wave begins.

required-evidence.evidence-1: The changelog contains a bounded v0.3.2 entry.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Release-facing docs describe the verified Telegram Desktop baseline without claiming call support.
required-evidence.evidence-3: GRACE artifacts are synchronized with the release truth.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The repository is clean before tag creation.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The remote baseline already contains v0.3.1 and the next tag is justified as v0.3.2.
required-evidence.evidence-6: No stale local v0.3.2 tag exists before the publish step.
required-evidence.evidence-7: The release truth preserves the verified Telegram Desktop SSH-forwarded path and explicitly leaves Telegram calls for a later UDP-capable phase.

Gate-Phase-16.goal: GitHub default-branch browsing reflects the same stable baseline that was already published as v0.3.2.

required-evidence.evidence-1: The branch sync target is explicitly bounded to the already released v0.3.2 baseline.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The tree is clean before the push to origin/master.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The push updates GitHub default-branch visibility and does not introduce a wider scope than the release baseline.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Post-push, origin/master resolves to the same commit as both local HEAD and v0.3.2.

Gate-Phase-17.goal: GitHub default-branch browsing is synchronized specifically to the released v0.3.2 commit and not to the newer local branch tip.

required-evidence.evidence-1: The corrective sync target is frozen to the released v0.3.2 commit.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The tree is clean before pushing the corrective master sync.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Post-push, origin/master resolves to the same commit as v0.3.2 and does not include the newer local planning commits.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The corrective push path uses the released refspec and not the local branch name.

Gate-Phase-18.goal: The project has a governed UDP-capable architecture for Telegram Desktop calls with bounded datagram verification and no overclaim beyond the tested setup.

commands.command-7: rg -n 'Telegram|call|voice|video|UDP|SOCKS5 UDP ASSOCIATE' README.md docs/OPERATORS.md docs/verification-plan.xml

required-evidence.evidence-1: The datagram contract, UDP ASSOCIATE ingress, association registry, datagram session manager, and datagram transport path are automated and green.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The Telegram calls wave defines separate signaling, media, reconnect, and failure packets rather than reusing the old TCP-only smoke envelope.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Public claims remain bounded to Telegram Desktop calls on the governed tested setup and do not imply universal unblock behavior.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The initial UDP-capable phase explicitly proves the WSS-backed datagram path and does not silently claim parity for any unimplemented datagram transport backend.

Gate-Phase-19.goal: The fresh host pair is redeployed with the UDP-capable baseline and yields bounded live Telegram Desktop calls evidence without widening the claim beyond the tested setup.

commands.command-1: ssh root@$N0WSS_SERVER_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'
commands.command-2: ssh root@$N0WSS_CLIENT_HOST 'uname -a && id && command -v bash && command -v systemctl && command -v ss'
commands.command-8: rg -n 'LV-010|voice|video|reconnect|UDP ASSOCIATE|go/no-go' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/OPERATORS.md README.md docs/development-plan.xml

required-evidence.evidence-1: The fresh-host baseline packet exists for both hosts before the calls wave begins.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The managed deploy and readiness packets prove the UDP-capable live baseline before the operator starts calls testing.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The operator receives one exact SSH-forward and SOCKS5 handoff for Telegram Desktop calls.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Voice, video, reconnect, and failure packets remain separable.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The final calls decision is bounded to the tested Telegram Desktop setup and does not imply universal unblock or broader client parity.
required-evidence.evidence-6: The gate does not allow ringing or answered-call UI alone to be treated as media-path success without UDP evidence.

Gate-Phase-20.goal: The repaired UDP ASSOCIATE ingress baseline is redeployed, rerun live evidence is captured, and the updated calls decision remains bounded to the tested Telegram Desktop setup.

required-evidence.evidence-1: The repaired deploy packet is explicitly tied to the post-fix baseline.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The rerun readiness packet proves whether UDP ASSOCIATE ingress now appears on the live client runtime.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The rerun voice and video packets stay separate from each other and from the original Phase-19 no-go packet.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The updated decision remains bounded to the tested Telegram Desktop setup and does not overclaim green call support unless the rerun evidence proves it.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The gate must distinguish “ingress defect fixed” from “calls fully green” if the rerun only moves the divergent block deeper.

Gate-Phase-21.goal: The local-client Telegram calls topology is defined, a local-topology calls wave is planned, and the future decision remains bounded to the tested setup.

required-evidence.evidence-1: The phase explicitly separates the future local-client topology from the older SSH-forwarded Desktop path.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The local-client readiness and local raw UDP ASSOCIATE proof are defined before any new calls wave is executed.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The future local voice and video packets stay separate and feed a topology-comparison packet.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The future calls decision remains bounded to the tested local-client topology only.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The phase forbids jumping straight to provider-blocking explanations before the local topology and governed datagram path are classified.

Gate-Phase-22.goal: The project has a bounded root-cause isolation plan for Telegram calls that distinguishes controlled datagram reachability, Telegram app behavior, and plausible external filtering.

commands.command-2: rg -n 'LV-013|udp_probe|packet capture|app behavior|remote media' docs/verification-plan.xml docs/development-plan.xml docs/knowledge-graph.xml docs/OPERATORS.md

required-evidence.evidence-1: The phase defines a controlled datagram probe outside Telegram UI before blaming the provider or network.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The phase defines a bounded workstation packet-capture or socket-evidence surface for live Telegram calls attempts.
required-evidence.evidence-3: The phase defines an explicit app-behavior classifier and a separate remote-media probe.
required-evidence.evidence-4: The final decision is bounded to naming the next architecture or workaround phase, not to claiming the root cause is already fixed.
required-evidence.evidence-5: The phase makes it impossible to collapse controlled-probe, live-app, and remote-media evidence into one blended conclusion without naming the first unresolved layer.

packet-template.phase-1-example: Config invalid-path test expected BLOCK_VALIDATE_CONFIGURATION reject marker but observed panic before marker emission; first divergent block is Config::loadConfig::BLOCK_VALIDATE_CONFIGURATION; next action is harden path validation and add deterministic error assertion.
packet-template.live-example-success-path: LiveFirstProxyRequest expected server and client startup anchors plus BLOCK_SELECT_TRANSPORT and BLOCK_PUMP_BIDIRECTIONAL, but observed client startup only and no server listener evidence; first divergent block is CliApp::run::BLOCK_START_APPLICATION on the server host; next action is repair server launch, TLS paths, or listener bind before repeating the smoke.
packet-template.live-example-auth-reject: LiveAuthReject expected BLOCK_VALIDATE_HANDSHAKE reject evidence and no surviving session, but observed successful transport selection and no reject marker; first divergent block is AuthPolicy::validateHandshake::BLOCK_VALIDATE_HANDSHAKE; next action is inspect live auth configuration and reject-path handling before any rerun.
packet-template.managed-deploy-example: ManagedDeployLayout expected one canonical artifact path plus aligned service and log targets, but observed deploy script targeting /opt/n0wss while the unit file still referenced a different path; first divergent block is DeployLayout::defineFilesystemLayout::BLOCK_DEPLOY_LAYOUT_ALIGNMENT; next action is realign deploy script, unit files, and operator docs before managed rollout.
packet-template.managed-restart-example: ManagedRestartRecovery expected BLOCK_COORDINATE_SHUTDOWN followed by a new managed readiness packet, but observed a restarted unit with no fresh startup anchors and a stale listener; first divergent block is CliApp::coordinateShutdown::BLOCK_COORDINATE_SHUTDOWN; next action is inspect systemd restart behavior, stale process cleanup, and readiness capture before rerun.
packet-template.release-gate-example: ReleaseGate030 expected a clean release-prep tree, v0.3.0 changelog entry, and green local checks, but observed a stale README or changelog and tracked dirty files; first divergent block is ReleaseGate030::runReleaseGate::BLOCK_RELEASE_GATE; next action is finish the remaining release-prep module steps before creating any tag.
packet-template.telegram-wave-example: TelegramProxyWave expected a Telegram client configured as a standard SOCKS5 consumer to reach connectivity through the local n0wss endpoint, but observed no SOCKS5 parse marker or no transport-selection marker after the app-side attempt; first divergent block is TelegramSmoke::runTelegramSmoke::BLOCK_TELEGRAM_SMOKE; next action is distinguish Telegram client misconfiguration from tunnel-side failure before rerun.
packet-template.telegram-launch-example: TelegramProxyWave reconnect expected a fresh Telegram app relaunch plus a new bounded packet, but observed local UI recovery instability while n0wss still emitted fresh SOCKS5 parse, transport-selection, bridge-pump, and accepted-handshake evidence; first divergent block is TelegramDesktop::relaunch::LOCAL_APP_RECOVERY; next action is preserve the reconnect success packet, classify the UI issue as app-side, and avoid misrouting the fix into tunnel runtime code.
packet-template.final-acceptance-example: FinalTelegramAcceptanceWave expected fresh-host baseline, managed readiness, a live SSH-forward, a basic Telegram Desktop connect packet, and separate media packets, but observed Telegram stuck on Connecting with no fresh SOCKS5 ingress marker; first divergent block is FinalTelegramHandoff::prepareFinalTelegramHandoff::BLOCK_FINAL_TELEGRAM_HANDOFF if the local forward is absent, otherwise Socks5Proxy::parseRequest::BLOCK_PARSE_SOCKS5_REQUEST; next action is verify the SSH-forward process and forwarded local bind first, then inspect remote client readiness before repeating only the dependent acceptance step.
packet-template.release-baseline-032-example: ReleaseBaseline032 expected a bounded v0.3.2 changelog entry, release-facing docs that preserve the verified Telegram Desktop SSH-forward path, a clean tree, and a remote baseline ending at v0.3.1, but observed docs or notes implying Telegram calls support or a stale local v0.3.2 tag before publication; first divergent block is ReleaseGate032::runReleaseGate032::BLOCK_RELEASE_GATE_0_3_2; next action is narrow the release truth back to the verified TCP or SOCKS5 envelope, remove the stale tag if present, and rerun only the release gate.
packet-template.branch-sync-example: GitHubMasterBaselineSync expected local HEAD and v0.3.2 to identify the same stable baseline while origin/master lagged behind, but observed a different local HEAD or a dirty tree before push; first divergent block is BranchSyncGate::runBranchSyncGate::BLOCK_BRANCH_SYNC_GATE; next action is restore the approved baseline identity and clean tree before syncing origin/master.
packet-template.released-master-sync-example: ReleasedCommitMasterSync expected v0.3.2 to remain the only allowed publish target while local HEAD had already moved beyond it, but observed a corrective push attempt still deriving its target from HEAD; first divergent block is ReleasedMasterGate::runReleasedMasterGate::BLOCK_RELEASED_MASTER_GATE; next action is freeze the released target explicitly and retry only the bounded push of v0.3.2:master.
packet-template.telegram-calls-example: TelegramCallsWave expected signaling green plus UDP ASSOCIATE, datagram transport, and UDP relay evidence for one voice or video call, but observed Telegram stuck after signaling while no UDP media markers appeared; first divergent block is Socks5Proxy::handleUdpAssociate::BLOCK_HANDLE_UDP_ASSOCIATE if no association is created, otherwise DatagramTransportSelector::selectTransport::BLOCK_SELECT_DATAGRAM_TRANSPORT or UdpEgressRelay::relayOutbound::BLOCK_RELAY_UDP_OUTBOUND depending on the first missing media marker; next action is repair the first divergent UDP layer before replaying the calls wave.
packet-template.live-calls-acceptance-example: TelegramCallsLiveAcceptanceWave expected fresh-host baseline, managed readiness, an explicit SSH-forward handoff, separate voice and video packets, reconnect evidence, and a bounded final decision, but observed a call symptom without the expected UDP markers or without a live forward; first divergent block is CallsHandoff::prepareCallsHandoff::BLOCK_CALLS_HANDOFF if the local path is ambiguous, otherwise Socks5Proxy::handleUdpAssociate::BLOCK_HANDLE_UDP_ASSOCIATE, DatagramTransportSelector::selectTransport::BLOCK_SELECT_DATAGRAM_TRANSPORT, or UdpEgressRelay::relayOutbound::BLOCK_RELAY_UDP_OUTBOUND depending on the first missing media marker; next action is repair the first divergent layer before replaying only the dependent live call step.
